Add fetch-mentions command to x-community.sh with OAuth 1.0a signing,

* fix: jq join drops tweets without matching user, strengthen tests

Replace generator-style jq join (silently drops tweets when author
missing from includes.users) with INDEX lookup that preserves all
tweets with 'unknown' fallbacks. Fix chmod ordering in agent docs.

Prevents cryptic exit code 5 when API returns malformed response.
Matches existing validation pattern in cmd_fetch_mentions and x_request.

* learning: jq generator-style joins silently drop unmatched records

commit.message:
add 6x6 and 7x7 cube support

Both use floor(n/2)=3 max layers: single (X), wide (Xw), triple-wide
(3Xw). Scramble lengths: 6x6=80, 7x7=100. Edge cycles follow the same
outer/inner1/inner2 pattern as 4x4/5x5. PuzzleType, RubiksCubeCSS
(grid-cols-6/7), and App dropdown updated accordingly.

commit.message:
Coupling audit: remove prohibited path, add 5 research-backed couplings

- REMOVE Disappointment->Desire direct collapse (research: independent constructs)
- ADD Trust->Price Sensitivity inverse (Erdem & Swait 2004, strength 0.005)
- ADD Loyalty->Price Sensitivity inverse (Krishnamurthi & Raj 1991, strength 0.003)
- ADD Trust->Desire modulation (Chaudhuri & Holbrook 2001, mod 0.5)
- ADD Satisfaction->Desire boost (Garbarino & Johnson 1999, boost 0.1)
- ADD Desire->Quality Expectations inflation (Brehm 1966, strength 0.0005)

All new couplings configurable via coupling.* config namespace.
Disappointment->Desire replaced by trust-mediated path (D->T->Des).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: sticky search state + recently viewed listings

Search state persistence:
- View mode (split/grid/list/map) now synced to URL (?view=) so it
  survives back-navigation
- Search query (q) synced to URL on every keystroke
- Scroll position saved to sessionStorage on unmount/navigation,
  restored when returning via browser back
- Mobile view mode preserved if user explicitly set one

Recently viewed listings:
- RecentlyViewedTracker records each listing visit to localStorage
  (id, slug, address, price, photo, beds, baths, type)
- RecentlyViewed component shows horizontal scrollable strip on
  search page with thumbnails, dismissible
- Max 12 items, newest first, deduped

commit.message:
Fix double-reply: track by chat name + message text, not just DOM IDs

WhatsApp re-renders DOM elements when navigating between chats, giving
messages new data-id attributes. The bot couldn't recognize already-replied
messages. Now tracks last replied message text per chat (with 2min window)
as the primary dedup, with DOM IDs as secondary guard.

commit.message:
fix: HoTs/Legals deals show requirement details instead of "Not Found"

- Fix broken zoho_id fallback in 3 API endpoints (deals/[id], deals/[id]/room,
  deals/[id]/requirement) — .single() returns error on no match, preventing
  fallback; switched to .maybeSingle() which returns null
- LiveDealDetails now redirects to RequirementDetails for deals not in
  Deal Room stages (HoTs, Legals, and earlier stages)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: variant resolution bug, tanwin validation, retirement priority, pipeline cap (#11)

Four fixes from health check investigation:

1. Variant resolution bug (sentence_selector.py): variant words that are due
   couldn't find their own sentences because the canonical→variant mapping
   prevented matching. Now checks both canonical and original lemma IDs.

2. Tanwin alif stripping (sentence_validator.py): accusative tanwin forms
   (e.g. سَعِيدًا) were rejected as unknown because after diacritic stripping
   the trailing alif seat remained (سعيدا ≠ سعيد). Added strip_tanwin_alif()
   to both validate_sentence and validate_sentence_multi_target.

3. Retirement priority inversion (update_material.py): never-shown sentences
   were retired FIRST (priority 0-1), causing freshly generated sentences to
   be killed before ever being used. Inverted: shown-stale retires first,
   never-shown sentences are now protected.

4. Pipeline cap raised from 800 to 1000 to match growing vocabulary (738
   active words needing ~1500 sentence slots).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update morning briefing — 10 Mar 2026
